text: Lufthansa provides specialized services for passengers with various types of impairments, including sensory impairments (hearing, visual, and cognitive) and reduced mobility. Passengers can register their assistance requirements online, and specific information is available for travel to the USA.

In the event of a damaged or lost mobility aid, Lufthansa provides the following options: 1) Lufthansa will cover the costs of repairing or replacing the device with one of comparable functionality and safety. 2) The passenger may arrange for repair or replacement through a provider of their choice; in this case, Lufthansa will manage the transport and pay the provider directly for the incurred costs.

section: Services for Passengers with Cognitive Impairment
text: To assist passengers with cognitive impairments in navigating airports, Lufthansa offers accompaniment throughout the airport, priority boarding and disembarking, and special instructions during delays or other travel-related issues. Assistance can be registered online. For safety, passengers must follow crew instructions during the flight, and the use of a traveling companion is suggested.

Lufthansa provides quick access to essential travel services, including viewing and amending flights, baggage information, check-in, airport services, travel briefings, customer services, Travel ID accounts, Miles & More, the Lufthansa app, and feedback/newsletter options.
The website offers navigation to flight offers, destinations, customer services, Travel ID accounts, Miles & More, help sections, the Lufthansa app, and newsletter subscriptions.

Lufthansa provides information regarding jobs and careers, corporate customers, travel agents, the Lufthansa Group for Business, and the corporate bonus programme. Users are advised that some of these links lead to external websites that may have different privacy, security, or accessibility policies.
Passengers who wish to travel with a companion can register them through the Lufthansa Service Centre.

Lufthansa participates in the Hidden Disabilities Sunflower programme to support passengers with invisible disabilities. Wearing the sunflower symbol is a discreet way to signal that a passenger may need additional time or assistance. While the symbol does not guarantee specific special services, staff will make every effort to ensure the passenger travels in comfort.

Lufthansa provides specialized assistance and information for various needs, including passengers with sensory impairments (hearing and visual), cognitive impairments, and passengers with reduced mobility, as well as specific information for travel to the USA.
Passengers requiring help at the airport can register for assistance online. Lufthansa provides accompaniment throughout the airport experience for those who have registered for support.
For the safety of all passengers, the instructions provided by the crew during the flight must be followed at all times.
Lufthansa recognizes that a travelling companion may be helpful for some passengers; companions can be registered via the Service Centre.
Passengers can register their specific assistance requirements online, with dedicated support available for sensory impairments (hearing and visual), cognitive impairments, and reduced mobility.
Lufthansa provides accompaniment for passengers throughout their entire time at the airport if required.
Lufthansa cabin crew provide separate, personal safety instructions to passengers who require them while on board.

Assistance dogs, which are specially trained to assist persons with visual, sensory, psychiatric, intellectual, or other cognitive impairments, may be taken on any Lufthansa route free of charge, regardless of breed or type.

To be permitted in the cabin, assistance dogs must be trained to obey commands and behave appropriately in public spaces (no barking, growling, or jumping). Additionally, dogs must not defecate in an uncontrolled manner in the cabin or at the gate to maintain hygiene and health standards.

If an assistance dog behaves inappropriately, Lufthansa reserves the right to refuse carriage or transport the animal in the cargo hold for a surcharge. The owner is liable for any damage and additional costs arising from the carriage of the dog.

Passengers can register their assistance dogs through the following channels: online, via the Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre (including the USA office), the Help Centre, or through a travel agency.

Passengers are responsible for ensuring that carrying an assistance dog as additional carry-on baggage complies with the current entry and exit regulations of all countries involved in their outbound and return journey.
Lufthansa provides assistance services for travelers with reduced mobility or sensory and cognitive impairments to ensure a safe, comfortable, and welcoming journey.
To ensure individual needs are met, passengers must notify Lufthansa of their assistance requirements no later than 48 hours before departure.
Groups traveling with their own mobility aids should notify Lufthansa of their specific care requirements as early as possible.

While Lufthansa strives to provide the best service for short-notice registrations, it cannot be guaranteed. Without advance registration, neither the requested assistance nor travel on the booked flight can be guaranteed.
Many airports provide their own assistance services. Passengers should check directly with the airports on their itinerary for specific details.
Passengers booking via telephone or a travel agency should inform Lufthansa of their individual needs before completing the booking. The contact number is 0800 8384267.
Lufthansa's online service for requesting assistance is available in English and German.
Passengers may request assistance via an online form, which requires a completed online booking.
When booking through a travel agency, passengers should inform the agency of their individual assistance needs before the booking is finalized to ensure the best possible service.
Lufthansa provides services for travelers with reduced mobility or sensory or cognitive impairments to ensure they feel safe, cared for, and welcome at all times.

The Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre (MOC) provides assistance and information via Fax (+49 - 69 69 - 68 36 77) and Email (specialservice@dlh.de). Operating hours are Monday to Friday 08:30-20:00, and Saturday, Sunday, and public holidays 08:30-16:30.
Travelers to and from the USA can contact the Medical Desk at Tel: +1-516-279-1768. Operating hours are Monday to Friday from 09:00 to 17:00.
Passengers booking tickets by telephone should inform Lufthansa of their individual assistance needs before completing the booking process.
Lufthansa provides an online service for requesting assistance, available in English and German. A completed online booking is required to access this service.
Passengers booking through a travel agency should inform the agency of their individual assistance needs before completing the booking to ensure the best possible service.

Lufthansa participates in the Hidden Disabilities Sunflower programme to support passengers with invisible disabilities. Wearing the sunflower symbol serves as a discreet way to indicate an invisible disability to staff.
Lufthansa provides specialized support and helpful tips to ensure that travel is as easy, safe, and comfortable as possible for passengers with visual impairments.
Passengers can register their support requirements online via the Lufthansa Service Centre. Specific information is also available for registering assistance dogs.

Lufthansa offers accompaniment throughout the airport if needed. At the gate, staff provide assistance with boarding and disembarking, as well as separate briefings regarding delays and other important travel announcements.

Onboard, cabin crew provide personalized safety instructions and assistance with cabin orientation. Crew members also explain the arrangement of food on the meal tray to help passengers identify their meal.
At the destination airport, Lufthansa staff provide assistance with exiting the airport or navigating connecting flights.
Support requirements and the use of assistance dogs can be registered online through the Lufthansa Service Centre.

section: Visual Impairment: Airport and Onboard Support
text: Lufthansa provides airport accompaniment and personalized safety instructions from the cabin crew for passengers with visual impairments.

The Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre (MOC) provides assistance via Fax at +49 - 69 69 - 68 36 77 and Email at specialservice@dlh.de. Operating hours are Monday to Friday from 08:30 to 20:00, and Saturday, Sunday, and public holidays from 08:30 to 16:30.
Travellers to and from the USA can contact the Medical Desk in the USA at Tel: +1-516-279-1768. Operating hours are Monday to Friday from 09:00 to 17:00.
For safety and well-being during a trip, some passengers may be required to travel with a companion.

A companion is required if a passenger: cannot understand safety instructions from the cabin crew, is unable to bring themselves to safety in the event of an emergency, or requires personal assistance on board or in the lounge.

Passengers with reduced mobility are carried in accordance with EU Regulation No. 1107/2006 (5 July 2006). Passengers travelling to or from the USA are carried in accordance with U.S. DOT provisions, Title 14, Code of Federal Regulations, Part 382 (effective 13 May 2009).

Complaint Resolution Officers (CRO) are available during opening hours at all USA destinations, as well as in Frankfurt and Munich, to assist passengers directly with problems. Crew members or ground staff can facilitate contact with a CRO.

Lufthansa provides specialized information and services for various accessibility needs, including accessible travel, healthy journeys, older travellers, global healthcare, doctor on board, and medical travel companions.

Lufthansa offers several options for registering assistance dogs, including carriage in the cabin, carriage in the cargo hold, and options for animals as excess baggage or additional carry-on baggage. Specific regulations apply to fighting dog breeds and snub-nosed animals.

section: Services for Sensory and Cognitive Impairments
text: Lufthansa provides specialized services for passengers with hearing or visual impairments, as well as those with cognitive disabilities, before, during, and after their flight.

To ensure the best support, passengers should notify Lufthansa of their assistance requirements at least 48 hours before departure. Requirements can be registered by telephone during the booking process, through a travel agency, or online after the booking has been completed.
Assistance requirements can be registered online or by calling 0800 - 83 84 267. Lufthansa cannot guarantee optimal assistance and support if registration is performed on short notice.

Passengers are generally free to choose the seat that best meets their needs, although some safety restrictions may apply. Most seats across the Lufthansa fleet feature moveable armrests to facilitate easier access.
For safety reasons, any seating device intended for use on board must be checked by Lufthansa's Medical Operation Centre in advance. Additionally, Lufthansa can transport up to two mobility aids.
Passengers are generally free to choose seats based on individual needs. However, for safety reasons, restrictions apply to all seats in the emergency exit rows regardless of aircraft type.
On B747-400 and B747-800 aircraft, seats on the upper deck are not suitable for passengers categorized as WCHS or WCHC because the upper deck is only accessible via a staircase.

For passengers using mobility aids in the A350-900 Allegris cabin, the following seats are not suitable for WCHC: First Class (1K); Business Class (Suites 2A-K and 8A-K, Privacy Seats 3A+K, 5A+K, 7A+K, 9A+K, 11A+K, and Extra Space Seats 3E, 5F, 9E, 11F). In Economy Class, rows 19-K, 26A-C, and 26H-K are not suitable for WCHC, WCHS, or WCHR.

section: Onboard Seat Features and Seating Devices
text: Most seats across the Lufthansa fleet feature movable armrests to facilitate easier access. If a passenger wishes to attach a personal seating device to their seat, Lufthansa must check the device beforehand for safety reasons.

section: Medical Operation Centre and Battery-Powered Wheelchairs
text: Passengers can register battery-powered wheelchairs by contacting the Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre.

section: Reporting Damaged or Delayed Mobility Aids
text: Lufthansa undertakes to handle wheelchairs and electric mobility scooters with care. If a mobility aid is damaged or delayed, passengers should report the incident immediately via the online reporting tool or at the destination airport's lost-property office.

section: Delivery Options for Delayed Mobility Aids
text: If a mobility aid arrives late, passengers can choose to either collect the device at the destination airport or have it delivered to a suitable location, such as a home or hotel.
Lufthansa will reimburse transport costs to or from the airport incurred due to a delayed mobility aid, provided that proof of expenses is submitted.

If a mobility aid is damaged during a flight, passengers are entitled to a loan wheelchair that meets their functional and safety requirements until their own device is returned, repaired, or replaced. If Lufthansa cannot provide a suitable loan device, passengers may organize one themselves and seek reimbursement for the costs within 30 days of submitting proof.

section: Repair or Replacement of Mobility Aids
text: In the event of a lost or damaged mobility aid, Lufthansa will cover the costs of repairing or replacing the device with one of comparable functionality and safety.
Lufthansa staff are available to provide assistance to passengers at their destination airport or when changing flights.
The Be My Eyes app is recommended as a tool to provide valuable assistance for people with impaired vision to make traveling easier.
Lufthansa provides helpful information and tips to help passengers with hearing impairments manage the specific challenges of flying to ensure a comfortable and stress-free journey.
Flying with a hearing impairment can present particular challenges; Lufthansa offers dedicated resources to assist these passengers.
Passengers can register their specific assistance requirements online through the Lufthansa website.
Lufthansa provides specific information and registration procedures for the carriage of assistance dogs.
If required, Lufthansa staff will accompany passengers throughout their entire time at the airport.
Passengers can register their assistance requirements online via the Lufthansa portal.
Information regarding the registration and carriage of assistance dogs is available through Lufthansa's online resources.
Lufthansa offers the service of accompanying passengers throughout their entire stay at the airport upon request.
Links to external websites may not follow the same privacy, security, or accessibility policies as the current site.

Lufthansa uses necessary cookies to operate its app and website. With user consent, additional cookies are used: Analytics cookies to evaluate performance and campaign efficiency, Personalisation cookies for a customised user experience, and Advertising cookies from third-party companies to deliver targeted ads.

Users may give, refuse, or withdraw consent for cookie usage at any time via the link at the bottom of each page. Further details on data collection and sharing are available in the Privacy and Cookie Policy.
The website provides navigation options for booking and preparation, discovering Lufthansa, and accessing assistance requirements and services for passengers with reduced mobility.

WCHR: Wheelchair assistance to the exit at the gate. This service is for passengers who can move independently on board the aircraft and use stairs, but require a wheelchair or other transport for long distances within the airport.

WCHS: Wheelchair assistance to the aircraft. This service is for passengers who can move independently on board the aircraft but cannot walk up or down stairs and require a wheelchair or other transport to navigate the airport.

WCHC: Wheelchair assistance for passengers unable to walk. This service is for passengers who can only travel with their own wheelchair and require assistance from airport arrival, through all boarding and disembarking phases (including onboard the aircraft), until arrival at the destination.
Passengers who wish to bring their own wheelchair will be provided with a wheelchair free of charge at the airport.
Regardless of breed or type, an assistance dog is specially trained to perform tasks or work for a person with an impairment.

Passengers can register their assistance dogs through the following channels: online, via the Help Centre, through a travel agency, or by emailing the Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre (including the dedicated centre for the USA).
When transporting an assistance dog in the cabin, the general advice for the carriage of animals in aircraft also applies.
Lufthansa distinguishes between assistance dogs on all flights outside the United States and assistance dogs on non-stop flights to or from the United States.
To ensure optimal preparation, assistance dogs should ideally be registered for cabin transport no later than 48 hours before departure.

Required documentation for assistance dogs includes a training certificate from a recognized training facility, a qualified dog trainer, or an accredited organization such as the Messerli Research Institute, Assistance Dogs International (ADI), Assistance Dogs Europe (ADEu), or the International Guide Dog Federation (IGDF). Additionally, the dog must wear a clearly visible identification tag. Please note that a DOT Form or a certificate of completed obedience training is not sufficient for travel outside the USA.
Passengers can transport their own foldable wheelchairs in the cargo hold free of charge. When doing so, passengers must specify the wheelchair's dimensions, weight, and any special features.
Passengers requiring wheelchair assistance at their place of departure or destination should inform the airline in good time, either during the booking process or afterwards.
Passengers are permitted to take up to two mobility aids.
Smaller aircraft may have size restrictions. A list of aircraft door dimensions by aircraft type is available for reference.
At most stations, foldable wheelchairs can be used until the gate. At check-in, these wheelchairs receive a 'Delivery at aircraft' tag and are loaded directly at the aircraft.

WCHR: Wheelchair assistance to the exit at the gate. This service is for passengers who can move independently on board the aircraft and use stairs, but require a wheelchair or other means of transport for long distances within the airport.

WCHS: Wheelchair assistance to the aircraft. This service is for passengers who can move independently on board the aircraft, but cannot walk up or down stairs and require a wheelchair or other means of transport to get around the airport.

WCHC: Wheelchair assistance for passengers unable to walk. This service provides assistance from arrival at the airport, through all phases of boarding and disembarking (including onboard the aircraft), until arrival at the destination. These wheelchairs receive a 'Delivery at aircraft' tag at check-in and are loaded directly at the aircraft.

Battery-powered wheelchairs may be subject to safety-related restrictions and regulations. Passengers must complete a specific form and send it to specialcases@dlh.de to verify if transport is possible.
Wheelchairs are provided free of charge at the airport for passengers who require them.
